content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	To see what's available **right now**:
31	
32	```
33	ls .archon/subagents/
34	```
35	
36	Read each `<name>.md`'s frontmatter (the first ~10 lines) to learn the subagent's purpose, write-domain hint, and directive expectations.
37	
38	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
39	
40	```
41	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
42	  --name <subagent-name> \
43	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
44	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
45	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
46	```
47	
48	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
49	
50	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
51	
52	Adding a new subagent or tool requires **no edits to this file**: drop a descriptor under `.archon/subagents/` (subagent) or a script under `.claude/tools/` (tool), and it surfaces via `ls`.
53	
54	## Key Files & Permissions
55	
56	All state files are in `.archon/`:
57	
58	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
59	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
60	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
61	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
62	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
63	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
64	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
65	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
66	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
67	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
68	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
69	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
70	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
71	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
72	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
73	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
74	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
75	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
76	
77	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
78	
79	## Protected Declarations
80	
81	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
82	
83	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
84	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
85	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
86	
87	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
88	
89	The blueprint uses two active markers.
90	
91	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
92	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
93	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
94	
95	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
96	
97	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
98	
99	## User Interaction
100	
101	Users provide hints in two places:
102	
103	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
104	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
105	
106	## Agent Roles
107	
108	### Plan Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
111	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
112	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
113	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
114	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
115	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
116	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
117	
118	### Prover Agent
119	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
120	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
121	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
122	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
123	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
124	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
125	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
126	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
127	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
128	
129	### Subagents
130	
131	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
132	
133	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
134	
135	```
136	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
137	```
138	
139	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
140	
141	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
145	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
146	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
147	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
148	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
149	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
150	
151	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
152	
153	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
154	
155	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
156	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
157	
158	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

67	## Current Objectives
68	
69	The dispatcher fans out one prover for the single file listed below.
70	
71	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` trailing sorry at L988 via Route B (named wrapper)
72

85	### Step 1 — define the wrapper `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'` + its R-linearity transport, then close L988
86	
87	**The wrapper is the iter-104 prover's + refactor agent's explicit
88	recommendation** for breaking the Fin-index mismatch between the
89	`Fin ((prev n) + 2)`-indexed named family and the `Fin (n + 1)`-indexed
90	call site sum. The wrapper is a NAMED top-level def whose head symbol
91	the discrimination tree can handle (unlike the anonymous-closure form
92	that defeated iter-099/100/101/103).
93	
94	**This iter explicitly lifts the iter-104 "no new top-level helpers"
95	constraint.** You ARE expected to add 1-2 top-level helpers — the
96	wrapper def + its R-linearity transport theorem. Mark each with an
97	`/- Iter-105 ... -/` doc-comment.
98	
99	#### Recommended structure
100	
101	**Helper 1 — wrapper def** (place after `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear`,
102	near L596, before `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` at L623):
103	
104	```lean
105	/-- Iter-105 wrapper: `Fin (n + 1)`-indexed view of
106	`cechCofaceMap_summand_family`. Bridges the natural Fin-index mismatch
107	between `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)` (the named family's
108	domain) and `Fin (n + 1)` (the call site sum's index) via `Fin.cast`
109	+ `eqToHom`. Used in `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`. -/
110	noncomputable def cechCofaceMap_summand_family'
111	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
112	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
113	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
114	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
115	        ModuleCat.of k
116	          (C.left.presheaf.obj
117	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) ⟶
118	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
119	        ModuleCat.of k
120	          (C.left.presheaf.obj
121	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) :=
122	  have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2 = n + 1 := by
123	    rcases n with _ | k
124	    · omega
125	    · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
126	  cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n (Fin.cast hRel.symm i) ≫
127	    eqToHom (by congr 1; funext j; congr 1; funext a; rw [hRel])
128	```
129	
130	**Helper 2 — R-linearity transport** (immediately after Helper 1):
131	
132	```lean
133	/-- Iter-105 wrapper R-linearity: transport of
134	`cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` to the `Fin (n + 1)`-indexed
135	wrapper. The R-action on both sides is unchanged from the named-family
136	version (same `RingHom.toModule` construction), so the transport is by
137	direct application + `≫`-distribution through `eqToHom`. -/
138	theorem cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear
139	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
140	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
141	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
142	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n)
143	    (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
144	    let R := Γ(C.left, U)
145	    let Z₁ := ... -- (same Z₁ as the named family theorem; Fin ((prev n) + 1))
146	    let Z₂ := ... -- Fin (n + 1) version
147	    letI perI₁ := ... -- same RingHom.toModule construction
148	    letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
149	    letI perI₂ := ... -- same RingHom.toModule construction
150	    letI h_mod_pi₂ : Module R (∀ i, Z₂ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
151	    let e₁ := (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
152	    let e₂ := (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv
153	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
154	      e₂ ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
155	        r • e₂ ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
156	  -- Proof sketch (~10 LOC):
157	  -- 1. Reconstruct letI module instances inside the body (same pattern as iter-104).
158	  -- 2. intro r y.
159	  -- 3. unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family' (or `show`-pivot).
160	  -- 4. The wrapper body = (named family at Fin.cast i) ≫ eqToHom.
161	  -- 5. ModuleCat.hom_comp pivots to LinearMap.comp.
162	  -- 6. Apply cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear hU s₀ n hn (Fin.cast _ i)
163	  --    on the left factor (= named family).
164	  -- 7. eqToHom is R-linear by construction (it's an Iso in ModuleCat).
165	  --    Use `LinearEquiv.map_smul` or direct `eqToHom` distribution lemmas.
166	  sorry
167	```
168	
169	**Step 1 closure** then uses these helpers at L988 (inside the
170	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body, in the per-summand `?hG` discharge frame
171	after `intro i _ r' y'`).
172	
173	#### Recommended L988 closure strategy
174	
175	Two routes — choose the one that fires first via `lean_multi_attempt`:
176	
177	**Route B.1 — switch the call site to the σ-binder lemma with the wrapper as G.**

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology

412	/-- Iter-087 (refactor): per-summand R-linear restriction-collapse. For nested
413	opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` in a scheme `C.left`, the chain of presheaf restrictions
414	`Γ(U) → Γ(W) → Γ(V)` collapses to the direct restriction `Γ(U) → Γ(V)`.
415	
416	Used in the proof of R-linearity of the Čech differential
417	(`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`): each per-summand restriction chain
418	`R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(C.left, V_j)` collapses to the
419	direct map `R → Γ(C.left, V_j)`, matching the per-i R-action induced by the
420	restriction algebra structure on the cochain factor.
421	
422	Extracted iter-087 from the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086) inside
423	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The body is the
424	2-tactic proof verified by the iter-086 prover. -/
425	lemma presheafMap_restrict_collapse
426	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
427	    {V W U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
428	    (h_VW : V ≤ W) (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U)
429	    (r : C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U)) :
430	    (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VW).op).hom
431	        ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_WU).op).hom r) =
432	      (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VU).op).hom r := by
433	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
434	  congr 1
435	
436	/-- Iter-102 refactor (cechcoface-named-family): extracted top-level family for the
437	sign-free Čech coface morphism components. The body is the `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...`
438	expression that appears (anonymously) in the post-iter-099 expanded
439	Čech differential inside `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`.
440	
441	Naming this family makes the head symbol a defined constant rather than
442	an anonymous closure, which unblocks the iter-099 `_sum_comp` /
443	`alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` call-site application:
444	- Miller unification on the G slot becomes trivial (G is given explicitly).
445	- The per-summand R-linearity hypothesis is a separate top-level theorem
446	  (`cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` below) that proves R-linearity
447	  of the named family at the binder level (HOU-free).
448	
449	The codomain index `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀` keeps the
450	definition independent of `hn : 0 < n` — it is the natural domain of
451	`(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom` for `i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)`.
452	The call site bridges to `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` via the existing `eqToHom`
453	slot fed into `E` of `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`. -/
454	noncomputable def cechCofaceMap_summand_family
455	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
456	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
457	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ)
458	    (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)) :
459	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
460	        ModuleCat.of k
461	          (C.left.presheaf.obj
462	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) ⟶
463	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀ =>
464	        ModuleCat.of k
465	          (C.left.presheaf.obj
466	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) :=
467	  Pi.lift fun i_1 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀ =>
468	    Pi.π (fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
469	            ModuleCat.of k
470	              (C.left.presheaf.obj
471	                (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a)))))
472	        (i_1 ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
473	      (toModuleKPresheaf C).map
474	        (Pi.lift fun x : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) =>
475	            Pi.π (fun a : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) =>
476	                    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i_1 a))
477	              ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x)).op

479	/-- Iter-102 refactor (cechcoface-named-family): per-summand R-linearity of the
480	sign-free Čech coface component `cechCofaceMap_summand_family`. The R-action on
481	the domain (`∏ᶜ Z₁`-side) is the Pi.module structure with each factor's
482	restriction-algebra structure; the R-action on the codomain (`∏ᶜ Z_int`-side) is
483	similarly the Pi.module structure with each factor's restriction-algebra
484	structure. The morphism factors through
485	`Pi.π Z_pre (i_1 ∘ δ i) ≫ presheaf.map _.op`, so R-linearity follows per
486	coordinate from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` + `Pi.lift_π_apply`.
487	
488	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-105 prover. Proof sketch (~15 LOC):
489	1. `intro r y`.
490	2. `apply LinearMap.ext`; `intro j`.   [goal: per-coordinate R-linearity]
491	3. `simp only [Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ...]`.
492	4. The composite at output coord `j` reads `(presheaf.map _).hom
493	   (y (j ∘ δ i))`. R-linearity follows from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`. -/
494	theorem cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear
495	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
496	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
497	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
498	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n)
499	    (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)) :
500	    let R := Γ(C.left, U)
501	    let Z₁ : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ModuleCat.{u} k :=
502	      fun i => ModuleCat.of k
503	        (C.left.presheaf.obj
504	          (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
505	    let Z_int : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀) → ModuleCat.{u} k :=
506	      fun i => ModuleCat.of k
507	        (C.left.presheaf.obj
508	          (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
509	    letI perI₁ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₁ i) := fun i => by
510	      apply RingHom.toModule
511	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
512	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
513	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
514	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
515	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
516	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
517	      exact h1.trans h2
518	    letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
519	    letI perI_int : ∀ i, Module R (Z_int i) := fun i => by
520	      apply RingHom.toModule
521	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
522	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
523	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
524	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
525	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
526	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
527	      exact h1.trans h2
528	    letI h_mod_pi_int : Module R (∀ i, Z_int i) := Pi.module _ _ _
529	    let e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, Z₁ i) :=
530	      (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
531	    let e_int : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, Z_int i) :=
532	      (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z_int).toLinearEquiv
533	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
534	      e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
535	        r • e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
536	  intro R Z₁ Z_int e₁ e_int
537	  -- iter-104 Step 1: reconstruct the letI module instances inside the body so
538	  -- the `r • y` HSMul synthesises against perI₁/perI_int (matching the goal).
539	  letI perI₁ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₁ i) := fun i => by
540	    apply RingHom.toModule
541	    refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
542	    let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
543	    have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
544	      basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
545	    have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
546	      basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
547	    exact h1.trans h2
548	  letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
549	  letI perI_int : ∀ i, Module R (Z_int i) := fun i => by
550	    apply RingHom.toModule
551	    refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
552	    let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
553	    have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
554	      basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
555	    have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
556	      basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
557	    exact h1.trans h2
558	  letI h_mod_pi_int : Module R (∀ i, Z_int i) := Pi.module _ _ _
559	  intro r y
560	  funext j'
561	  -- iter-104 Step 2: pivot the e_int X j' goal via Pi.smul_apply, then convert
562	  -- to (Pi.π Z_int j').hom X via Iso.toLinearEquiv_apply + ModuleCat.piIsoPi.
563	  simp only [Pi.smul_apply]
564	  show (Pi.π Z_int j').hom ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom
565	      (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
566	    r • (Pi.π Z_int j').hom ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm y))
567	  -- iter-104 Step 3: unfold the named family + apply Pi.lift_π_apply twice +
568	  -- ConcreteCategory.comp_apply to peel off the outer Pi.π and split the ≫.
569	  unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family
570	  simp only [Limits.Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
571	  -- iter-104 Step 4: (Pi.π Z₁ a).hom (e₁.symm Z) = Z a via piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι.
572	  have hSym : ∀ (Z : (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i))
573	      (a : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀),
574	      (Pi.π Z₁ a).hom (e₁.symm Z) = Z a := by
575	    intro Z a
576	    exact ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply Z₁ a Z
577	  rw [hSym, hSym]
578	  -- iter-104 Step 5: expand (r • y a) and (r • F y) via RingHom.toModule_smul
579	  -- (definitional unfolding of perI₁/perI_int).
580	  simp only [Pi.smul_apply, RingHom.toModule_smul]
581	  -- iter-104 Step 6 (FINAL): the remaining goal is
582	  --   (presheaf.map Pl.op).hom (f₁(r) * y_val) = f₂(r) * (presheaf.map Pl.op).hom y_val
583	  -- via (presheaf.map Pl.op).hom being a ring hom (map_mul) +
584	  -- presheafMap_restrict_collapse identifying the chain f₁ ∘ (presheaf.map Pl.op) = f₂.
585	  -- Closed via term-level Eq.trans + congrArg to bypass HMul-synth issues on the
586	  -- output ring (W₂ side) that blocked `rw [(...).hom.map_mul]` tactic-level.
587	  set Pl : (∏ᶜ fun a : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) =>
588	      basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j' a)) ⟶
589	        (∏ᶜ fun x : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) =>
590	            basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j' ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x))) :=
591	    Pi.lift fun x => Pi.π _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x) with hPl_def
592	  exact ((C.left.presheaf.map Pl.op).hom.map_mul _ _).trans
593	    (congrArg (· * (C.left.presheaf.map Pl.op).hom
594	        (y (j' ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
595	      (presheafMap_restrict_collapse _ _ _ r))
596	
597	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
598	/-- Iter-096 (refactor extraction): structural lemma — if each summand
599	`F i` is R-linear (with respect to the LinearEquivs `e₁`, `e₂`), then
600	so is `∑ i ∈ s, F i`. The summand family `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)` is named
601	abstractly, so applying this lemma to the Čech alternating sum
602	`∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` only requires Miller-pattern
603	unification `F := fun i ↦ <literal summand>` — no HOU on the
604	nested-i body.
605	
606	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-097 prover. Proof sketch:
607	induct on the finset `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Base case is
608	the empty sum = 0, which is trivially R-linear via the `ModuleCat`
609	zero morphism. Cons step: `F i + ∑ j ∈ s', F j` is R-linear because
610	R-linearity is preserved under addition (`ModuleCat.hom_add` plus
611	R-linearity of each summand).
612	
613	**Refactor note (iter-096):** The directive also requested a companion
614	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand` that bakes the Čech summand literal
615	into its signature. That signature triggers a whnf timeout at the
616	`Pi.lift (fun x ↦ Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i)
617	.toOrderHom x))`-elaboration step, because Lean attempts to unify
618	`Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` with `Fin n → ↥s₀` through
619	the heavy `ComplexShape.prev` definition. We omit that helper in iter-096;
620	the abstract structural lemma below suffices to give the iter-097 prover a
621	HOU-friendly hook (instantiate `F := fun i ↦ <Čech summand>` and discharge
622	per-summand R-linearity inline). -/
623	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
624	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
625	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
626	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
627	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
628	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
629	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
630	    (F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
631	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
632	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
633	    (hF : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
634	      e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
635	        r • e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
636	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
637	      e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
638	        r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
639	  -- iter-097 Step 1: induct on `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Empty case
640	  -- collapses via `ModuleCat.hom_zero`; cons step distributes `Finset.sum_cons`
641	  -- + `ModuleCat.hom_add` + `LinearMap.add_apply` + `map_add` simultaneously
642	  -- on both sides via `simp only`, then applies `hF` for the head and `ih`
643	  -- for the tail, finishing with `smul_add`. HOU-free because `F` is a binder.
644	  revert hF
645	  induction s using Finset.cons_induction with
646	  | empty =>
647	    intro _hF r y
648	    simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply,
649	      map_zero, smul_zero]
650	  | cons i s' hi ih =>
651	    intro hF r y
652	    simp only [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
653	    rw [hF i (Finset.mem_cons_self i s')]
654	    rw [ih (fun j hj => hF j (Finset.mem_cons.mpr (Or.inr hj))) r y]
655	    rw [smul_add]

657	/-- Iter-098 refactor: sum-through-composition extension of
658	`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`. The existing lemma's single morphism
659	family `F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂))` cannot absorb an intermediate
660	`eqToHom` because instantiating `F := fun i ↦ G i ≫ eqToHom`
661	re-introduces a `Fin (...) → ↑s₀` vs `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` unification
662	problem outside tactic state (the iter-097 Attempt 5 dead-end). This
663	parallel lemma splits the morphism into a family `G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶
664	(∏ᶜ Z_int))` plus a single `E : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)`, so the
665	`eqToHom` slot at the call site lands in `E` (an independent
666	elaboration slot) rather than inside the family.
667	
668	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-099 prover. Proof sketch:
669	`rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]` to convert
670	`(∑ G i) ≫ E` to `∑ (G i ≫ E)`, then `exact
671	alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y`.
672	Both rewrites are HOU-free at the signature level because `G` is a
673	binder, not a literal with nested-`i` Pi.lift body. -/
674	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
675	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
676	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
677	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
678	    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
679	    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
680	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
681	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
682	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
683	    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
684	    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
685	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
686	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
687	    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
688	      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
689	        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
690	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
691	      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
692	        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
693	  -- iter-099 Step 1: distribute `Preadditive.sum_comp` (HOU-free since `G`
694	  -- and `E` are lemma binders, not literals), then apply
695	  -- `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on the family `fun i ↦ G i ≫ E`.
696	  intro r y
697	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]
698	  exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y
699

700	/-- Iter-102 refactor: zsmul-through-composition extension of
701	`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`. Adds a per-summand sign
702	`σ : ι' → ℤ` as a separate binder so that the per-summand R-linearity
703	hypothesis `hG` is about the **sign-free** composite `G i ≫ E` rather than
704	the sign-wrapped `(σ i • G i) ≫ E`. The body internally handles the
705	sign-to-R-action commutativity at the binder level, where Lean's
706	discrimination tree has no anonymous-closure to choke on.
707	
708	This unblocks the `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` `?hG` discharge: the iter-099
709	call site bakes `(-1)^↑i` into `?G`, leading to a discharge that requires
710	`Preadditive.zsmul_comp` on a Pi.lift with anonymous-closure codomain —
711	three consecutive prover lanes (iter-099, iter-100, iter-101) failed on
712	this class. With the new lemma, the discharge is about the sign-free
713	`Pi.lift_thing_i ≫ eqToHom`, which is closed via
714	`presheafMap_restrict_collapse` per coordinate.
715	
716	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-103 prover. Proof sketch (~5-10 lines):
717	1. `intro r y`.
718	2. `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s (fun i ↦ σ i • G i) E]` to distribute
719	   composition through the sum. The pattern `?f ≫ ?g` with `?f := σ i • G i`
720	   is HOU-free because the *family* `fun i ↦ σ i • G i` is a binder
721	   structurally identical to the family `G` in the iter-099 `_sum_comp`
722	   body.
723	3. `simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` to pull each `σ i • _` outside the
724	   composition. **HOU-free** because `G` is a binder; `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`
725	   pattern `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g` matches with `?f := G i` (typed variable).
726	4. Apply `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` with `F i := σ i • (G i ≫ E)` and
727	   a derived per-summand hypothesis `hF` proved inline from `hG` using
728	   `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` + `smul_comm` + `hG`. All three steps work at
729	   the binder level.
730	-/
731	theorem alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
732	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
733	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
734	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
735	    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
736	    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
737	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
738	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
739	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
740	    (σ : ι' → ℤ)
741	    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
742	    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
743	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
744	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
745	    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
746	      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
747	        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
748	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
749	      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
750	        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
751	  -- iter-103 Path B: reduce to `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on the family
752	  -- `fun i ↦ σ i • (G i ≫ E)` (single morphism per `i`, no nested closure).
753	  -- HOU-free because `G`, `E`, `σ` are binder-level typed variables.
754	  intro r y
755	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s (fun i ↦ σ i • G i) E]
756	  simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
757	  -- Goal: e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • (G i ≫ E)).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
758	  --       r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • (G i ≫ E)).hom (e₁.symm y))
759	  -- Use `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` with the per-summand R-linearity derived
760	  -- from `hG` by extracting `σ i` def-equally and `smul_comm`-ing with `r`.
761	  exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ σ i • (G i ≫ E)) e₁ e₂
762	    (fun i hi r y => by
763	      -- (σ i • (G i ≫ E)).hom = σ i • (G i ≫ E).hom by ModuleCat.hom_zsmul (rfl)
764	      -- and (σ i • L) z = σ i • L z by LinearMap.smul_apply (rfl). Both rfl at
765	      -- the BINDER LEVEL since (G i ≫ E) is a typed variable (no closure).
766	      show e₂ (σ i • (G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
767	        r • e₂ (σ i • (G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
768	      rw [map_zsmul e₂ (σ i), map_zsmul e₂ (σ i),
769	          hG i hi r y, smul_comm (σ i) r])
770	    r y

831	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤

838	      e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
839	        r • e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm y)) := by
840	  intro R K₀ scK₀ Z₁ Z₂ e₁ e₂ r y

849	    have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤

859	    have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤

897	      ∀ {M N : ModuleCat.{u} k} {ι : Type}
898	        (f : ι → (M ⟶ N)) (s : Finset ι),
899	        ((∑ i ∈ s, f i) : M ⟶ N).hom = ∑ i ∈ s, (f i).hom :=
900	    @fun M N ι => ModuleCat.hom_sum (M := M) (N := N) (R := k)
901	  -- S6 step (b) [iter-093]: per-application form of `ModuleCat.hom_sum`.

903	      ∀ (F : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
904	        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
905	        ((∑ i, F i) : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)).hom z =

907	    intro F z

915	  rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]

917	  -- `Preadditive.sum_comp` + `ModuleCat.hom_sum` + `LinearMap.sum_apply`
918	  -- into a single per-application distribution. Proved with FREE `G`/`E`/`z`
919	  -- (not bound to the goal's summand) so the proof itself avoids HOU. The
920	  -- BLOCKER remaining is that `rw [key₂]` / `simp_rw [key₂]` on the post-(b')

925	  have key₂ :
926	      ∀ (G : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
927	        (E : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i)))
928	        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
929	        ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z := by
930	    intro G E z
931	    rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
932	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum (fun i => G i ≫ E) Finset.univ]
933	    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (G i ≫ E).hom) z

997	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
998	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
999	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
1000	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
1001	schemes in our setting.
1002	
1003	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
1004	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
1005	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
1006	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
1007	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
1008	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
1009	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
1010	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
1011	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
1012	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
1013	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
1014	
1015	The structural skeleton is:
1016	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
1017	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
1018	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
1019	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
1020	
1021	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
1022	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
1023	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
1024	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
1025	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
1026	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
1027	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
1028	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
1029	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
1030	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
1031	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
1032	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
1033	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
1034	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
1035	
1036	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
1037	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/

1039	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
1040	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
1041	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
1042	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
1043	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
1044	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
1045	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
1046	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
1047	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
1048	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
1049	    -- The goal is now
1050	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
1051	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
1052	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
1053	    --
1054	    -- ===========================================================================
1055	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
1056	    -- ===========================================================================
1057	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
1058	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
1059	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
1060	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
1061	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
1062	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
1063	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
1064	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
1065	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
1066	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
1067	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
1068	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
1069	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
1070	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
1071	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
1072	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
1073	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
1074	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
1075	      intro f
1076	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
1077	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
1078	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
1079	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.

# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-105 prover report

## Summary

- **Sorries**: 6 (same as iter-104). File compiles end-to-end.
- **L1147 (was L988) `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` trailing sorry**: **PARTIAL** — wrapper
  infrastructure added (Helper 1 + Helper 2 fully proved), partial proof attempt at
  the sorry exposes the per-summand R-linearity gap with the wrapper. The remaining
  gap is the per-coordinate eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport identification.
- **Iter-104 closures preserved byte-for-byte**: `cechCofaceMap_summand_family`
  L454-L477, `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` L494-L595 untouched.

## Step 1 — wrapper helpers (DONE, both compile, fully closed)

### Helper 1 — `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'` (wrapper def, L605–L633)

Defined as a direct `Pi.lift` over `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` with `Fin.cast` applied to
the inner-index translation. The body matches the named family's structure at
each coordinate via `(j_new ∘ Fin.cast hRel) ∘ δ_(Fin.cast hRel.symm i).toOrderHom`.

```lean
noncomputable def cechCofaceMap_summand_family'
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ => ...) ⟶
    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀ => ...) :=
  have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2 = n + 1 := by
    rcases n with _ | k
    · omega
    · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
  Pi.lift fun j_new : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
    Pi.π (...) ((j_new ∘ Fin.cast hRel) ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (Fin.cast hRel.symm i)).toOrderHom) ≫
      (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x => Pi.π (...) (Fin.cast hRel ((SimplexCategory.δ (Fin.cast hRel.symm i)).toOrderHom x))).op
```

### Helper 2 — `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear` (R-linearity, L639–L728)

Mirrors the iter-104 proof structure of `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear`,
but with Fin (n + 1) outer index and Fin.cast on the inner δ-composition. Key
steps:
1. Reconstruct letI module instances (perI₁, h_mod_pi₁, perI₂, h_mod_pi₂).
2. `intro r y; funext j_new`.
3. `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`, `show` pivot to `(Pi.π Z₂ j_new).hom`.
4. `unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family'`, then `simp [Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]`.
5. Apply `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply` twice to peel off `e₁.symm`.
6. `simp [Pi.smul_apply, RingHom.toModule_smul]`.
7. `set Pl := Pi.lift fun x => Pi.π _ (Fin.cast hRel (δ x))` with `hPl_def`.
8. `exact (map_mul _).trans (congrArg (· * ...) (presheafMap_restrict_collapse _ _ _ r))`.

The proof closes fully (zero sorries in the wrapper R-linearity body).

## Step 1 closure on L1147 — PARTIAL (sorry retained)

At L1133–L1147 a structured partial proof is in place:

```lean
have hRel' : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2 = n + 1 := by omega
have h_wrap := cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear hU s₀ n hn (Fin.cast hRel' i)
have h_wrap_pt := congrFun (h_wrap r' y') j'
simp only [Pi.smul_apply] at h_wrap_pt
-- h_wrap_pt now has:
--   (e₂ (wrapper.hom (e₁.symm (r' • y')))) j' = r' • (e₂ (wrapper.hom (e₁.symm y'))) j'
sorry
```

### Remaining gap (precise)

The wrapper R-linearity equation `h_wrap_pt` provides:
```
(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom ((wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)).hom (e₁.symm (r' • y'))) =
  r' • (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom ((wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)).hom (e₁.symm y'))
```
(after `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` unfolds `e₂ X j' = (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom X`).

The L1147 goal involves
`(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (eqToHom.hom (((-1)^↑i • F_at_i).hom z))` where F_at_i is the
literal Pi.lift (definitionally `cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i`). The gap
is reducing the goal LHS to h_wrap_pt's LHS:

1. **Pull `(-1)^↑i •` out**: `((-1)^↑i • F).hom z = (-1)^↑i • F.hom z` (via
   `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` + `LinearMap.smul_apply`). Then push through eqToHom
   and Pi.π Z₂ j' (both k-linear, hence ℤ-linear, via `map_zsmul`).

2. **Commute `(-1)^↑i •` past `r' •`** via `smul_comm` on Z₂ j' (R-module
   with ℤ acting as iterated addition).

3. **CORE GAP**: identify
   `(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (eqToHom.hom (F_at_i.hom z))`
   with
   `(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom ((wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)).hom z)`.

   This is morphism-level equality `F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer = wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)`
   (or equivalently, their evaluations at coordinate j' are equal). Both are
   Pi.lifts into ∏ᶜ Z₂ — for the wrapper, by construction; for `F ≫ eqToHom`,
   via the eqToHom transport. Per-coord both give `(presheaf.map _.op).hom (z (some_index))`
   where `some_index` is `j_int ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom` for the named family vs
   `(j' ∘ Fin.cast hRel) ∘ δ_(Fin.cast hRel.symm (Fin.cast hRel i)).toOrderHom`
   for the wrapper at `Fin.cast hRel' i`. Modulo `Fin.cast_cast` round-trip
   (`Fin.cast hRel.symm (Fin.cast hRel i) = i`) and the choice `j_int = j' ∘ Fin.cast hRel`,
   these indices coincide.

   Mathlib does **not** directly expose eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport for **object**
   equality eqToHom (only `Pi.π_comp_eqToHom` for index equality). Therefore the
   transport requires either:
   - explicit `convert` + `congr` on the eqToHom proof object (3-deep), or
   - a custom morphism-equality lemma proved by Pi.lift_ext + per-coord match.

   `convert h_wrap_pt using 2/3` was probed and produces 2 sub-goals of the
   form (LHS-of-goal) = (LHS-of-h_wrap_pt) and similarly for RHS — these are
   exactly the per-coord eqToHom transport identifications.

## Recommendations for iter-106

The wrapper infrastructure (Helper 1 + Helper 2) is in place and proved. The
remaining gap is purely the eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport identification at
coordinate j'. Three concrete routes:

1. **Add a morphism-equality lemma** `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_comp_eqToHom_eq`
   stating `F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer = cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn (Fin.cast hRel' i)`,
   proved by `Pi.lift_ext` (or `Limits.limit_ext` from Mathlib) + per-coord match
   using `Pi.lift_π_apply` on both sides. The per-coord goal reduces to a
   `presheafMap` equality that follows from `Fin.cast_cast` + `funext`.

2. **rcases-on-n approach inside the per-summand discharge**: do
   `rcases n with _ | m` AFTER intro at L1096 (`intro i _ r' y'`). In the
   `m + 1` case, after `simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel] at *`,
   the eqToHom should collapse (Fin types align, eqToHom of refl = identity).
   Then `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` applies directly. Was probed
   but the simp at * sometimes fails because (prev (m+1)) doesn't auto-simplify
   in all term positions.

3. **Use `convert h_wrap_pt using 3` + handle eqToHom sub-goals**: The two
   resulting sub-goals are exactly the per-coord transport. Each can be closed
   by `Pi.lift_π_apply` + `Fin.cast_cast` (round-trip) + `funext` + `rfl`.

Route 1 is the cleanest; Route 3 is the most direct from the current state.

## File state verification (iter-105)

| Metric | Before (iter-104) | After (iter-105) |
|---|---|---|
| Sorry count | 6 | 6 |
| Compiles? | ✅ | ✅ |
| Errors | none | none |
| New helpers | — | 2 (wrapper def + R-linearity, ~160 LOC) |
| L988→L1147 sorry | bare | structured partial proof |
| New axioms | 0 | 0 |
| `cechCofaceMap_summand_family` body | unchanged | unchanged |
| `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` body | unchanged | unchanged |

Sorries (post-iter-105): **L1147** (cechCofaceMap_pi_smul trailing — partial),
L1239 (substep a extra-degeneracy, off-limits), L1563 (was L1404), L1591 (was
L1432), L1781 (was L1622), L1810 (was L1651). All non-target sorries are
unchanged from iter-104 (offset +159 lines from helper additions).

## Final `lean_diagnostic_messages` output

`severity=error`: `[]` (no errors). Only style/linter warnings remain
(show→change, long-line, unused-variable, flexible-tactic — all pre-existing
in the iter-104 baseline).
